report_metadata.cancer_type: Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma of the Sublingual Gland

treatments.name: Wide Local Excision (Floor of Mouth Resection) with Lingual Nerve Sacrifice

evidence_level.rationale: Surgical excision is the universally accepted primary treatment for sublingual gland ACC based on decades of retrospective institutional series and NCCN guidelines

survival_benefit.rationale: Surgery with clear margins achieves 5-year OS of 70-81% for T2N0 disease; 10-year LC of 84% when combined with adjuvant RT

accessibility.rationale: Widely available at head and neck surgical centers; standard procedure for floor of mouth malignancies

safety_profile.rationale: Lingual nerve sacrifice causes ipsilateral tongue numbness and taste loss; floor of mouth reconstruction may be needed; swallowing rehabilitation required

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal applicability regardless of molecular profile; perineural invasion mandates aggressive surgical margins including nerve sacrifice

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete surgical excision of the sublingual gland tumor with en bloc floor of mouth resection and sacrifice of the lingual nerve due to high propensity for perineural invasion in ACC. Achieves local control through physical removal of all gross and microscopic disease.

key_evidence.study_name: Malignant sublingual gland tumors: retrospective clinicopathologic study

notable_side_effects: Ipsilateral lingual nerve deficit (numbness, taste loss)

treatments.name: Adjuvant Radiation Therapy (Photon-based IMRT) for Perineural Invasion

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple retrospective series and NCCN guidelines strongly recommend adjuvant RT for ACC with perineural invasion; 10-year LC of 84% with postoperative RT

survival_benefit.rationale: Adjuvant RT improves 5-year OS from 72.5% to 82.4% vs surgery alone; locoregional control benefit is well-established even if OS benefit is debated

accessibility.rationale: IMRT widely available at radiation oncology centers; standard adjuvant treatment for ACC with PNI

safety_profile.rationale: Mucositis, xerostomia, osteoradionecrosis risk, fibrosis; long-term dental complications; floor of mouth tissue tolerance concerns

biomarker_match.rationale: PNI-positive status is the primary indication; universally applicable to this patient's presentation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: High-energy photon radiation delivered via intensity-modulated technique to the surgical bed and at-risk perineural pathways. Targets residual microscopic disease along nerves, which is the primary route of ACC spread. Typical dose 60-66 Gy in 30-33 fractions.

key_evidence.study_name: Impact of Tumor Site and Adjuvant Radiotherapy on Survival of Patients with ACC: SEER Database Analysis

treatments.name: Adjuvant Proton Beam Radiation Therapy

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple institutional series demonstrate excellent outcomes; 3-year LC 95%, PFS 81%, OS 96% in recent multi-institutional study

survival_benefit.rationale: Superior locoregional control vs photon RT with 3-year LC 95% and OS 96%; reduced late toxicity may improve long-term outcomes over 20+ year surveillance

accessibility.rationale: Limited to major proton therapy centers; increasing availability but still not universally accessible; requires referral

safety_profile.rationale: Favorable toxicity profile compared to photon RT; Bragg peak spares adjacent normal tissue; less xerostomia and mucositis

biomarker_match.rationale: Universally applicable to ACC with PNI; particularly valuable for floor of mouth location where dose conformality spares critical structures

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Charged particle beams exploit the Bragg peak to deliver maximum dose to the tumor bed while minimizing exit dose to surrounding normal tissues. Particularly advantageous for ACC given the need for high doses along perineural pathways near critical structures in the floor of mouth.

key_evidence.study_name: Long-Term Outcomes Following Definitive or Adjuvant Proton Radiotherapy for ACC

notable_side_effects: Mucositis (generally less severe than photon RT)

treatments.name: AL101 (Gamma-Secretase Inhibitor) for NOTCH1-Activated ACC

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II ACCURACY trial data: 12% ORR (9/77 PR), 69% DCR; median PFS 4.7 months for AL101; represents best NOTCH-targeted data in ACC

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 4.7 months; median response duration 9.9 months in responders; disease stabilization in majority but modest objective responses

accessibility.rationale: Available only through clinical trials; limited to centers participating in ACCURACY and successor studies

safety_profile.rationale: Most common AEs: diarrhea 78% (G3 10%), fatigue 67% (G3 6%), nausea 60% (G3 7%); GI toxicity is class effect of gamma-secretase inhibitors

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets the NOTCH1 activation present in this patient; NOTCH1-activated ACC is the specific population studied

treatments.mechanism_of_action: AL101 is a selective gamma-secretase inhibitor that blocks the proteolytic cleavage of NOTCH receptors, preventing release of the NOTCH intracellular domain (NICD) and downstream transcriptional activation. In NOTCH1-activated ACC, this disrupts the oncogenic signaling driving tumor growth.

key_evidence.study_name: ACCURACY: Phase 2 trial of AL101 in R/M ACC harboring Notch activating mutations

treatments.name: Lenvatinib (Multi-Targeted TKI)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial demonstrated 15.6% ORR and 17.5-month median PFS; best outcomes among VEGFR-targeting TKIs in ACC; NCCN-listed option

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 17.5 months represents clinically meaningful disease control; longest PFS among TKIs studied in ACC

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for thyroid cancer; off-label use for ACC supported by NCCN guidelines; oral medication widely available

safety_profile.rationale: 71% experienced grade 3+ AEs in combination studies; hypertension, fatigue, diarrhea, proteinuria are common; dose reductions frequently needed

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker required; VEGFR pathway broadly active in ACC but not specific to MYB-NFIB or NOTCH1 alterations

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFR-alpha, RET, and KIT. Inhibits tumor angiogenesis and directly targets tumor cell signaling through multiple receptor tyrosine kinase pathways active in ACC.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Study of Lenvatinib in Progressive R/M ACC

treatments.name: Rivoceranib (Apatinib) - Selective VEGFR2 Inhibitor

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II RM-202 trial with 72 evaluable patients showed 15.3% ORR by investigator and 9.7% by BIRC; median PFS 9.0 months

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 9.0 months; median DOR 14.9 months by investigator assessment; meaningful disease control in a disease with limited options

accessibility.rationale: Approved in some countries for gastric cancer; availability for ACC varies by region; clinical trial access expanding

safety_profile.rationale: 70% grade 3+ TRAEs; hypertension 42.5%, stomatitis 7.5%; significant toxicity burden requires close monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker requirement; targets VEGFR2 broadly expressed in ACC vasculature but not specific to MYB-NFIB or NOTCH1

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Rivoceranib (formerly apatinib) is a highly selective oral VEGFR2 tyrosine kinase inhibitor that potently blocks tumor angiogenesis by inhibiting VEGF-stimulated endothelial cell migration, proliferation, and survival signaling.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Trial of Rivoceranib for R/M ACC (RM-202)

treatments.name: Emiltatug Ledadotin (XMT-1660) - B7-H4 Antibody-Drug Conjugate

evidence_level.rationale: Phase I dose-escalation with preliminary ACC-I data showing unprecedented 55.6% ORR; early data but highest response rate ever seen in ACC

survival_benefit.rationale: 55.6% ORR in ACC-I patients is the highest response rate ever reported for any drug in ACC; potential for meaningful survival benefit

accessibility.rationale: Phase I trial only; very limited access; requires B7-H4 expression confirmation and enrollment at trial sites

safety_profile.rationale: No grade 4/5 TRAEs observed; most common: AST increase 38%, proteinuria 31%, nausea 29%, fatigue 28%; generally well tolerated

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires B7-H4 (VTCN1) expression; B7-H4 is highly prevalent in ACC-I subtype; needs confirmation of expression in this patient's tumor

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Emiltatug ledadotin is a B7-H4-directed Dolasynthen antibody-drug conjugate that delivers a cytotoxic payload specifically to tumor cells expressing B7-H4 protein. Upon internalization, the drug is released intracellularly to induce cell death.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 1 dose escalation of emiltatug ledadotin in advanced solid tumors

treatments.name: Carbon Ion Radiation Therapy (CIRT)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple institutional series demonstrate 5-year LC 75%, OS 63%; emerging prospective data from ACCO trial; overcomes ACC radioresistance with high-LET beams

survival_benefit.rationale: 5-year LC 75% and OS 63% for all comers; superior biological effectiveness for radioresistant ACC histology; valuable for recurrence/re-irradiation

accessibility.rationale: Extremely limited availability; only a few centers worldwide (Japan, Germany, Italy, China); requires international referral for most patients

safety_profile.rationale: Favorable toxicity profile; no grade 3/4 toxicities in some series; Bragg peak and high-LET provide excellent dose conformality

biomarker_match.rationale: Universally applicable to ACC regardless of molecular profile; overcomes inherent radioresistance characteristic of ACC

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Carbon ion beams combine high linear energy transfer (LET) with Bragg peak dose distribution. The high-LET causes complex, irreparable DNA double-strand breaks that overcome ACC radioresistance, while the Bragg peak confines dose delivery to the target volume with minimal exit dose.

key_evidence.study_name: Biological Rationale and Clinical Evidence of Carbon Ion RT for ACC: Narrative Review

treatments.name: Cisplatin-Doxorubicin-Cyclophosphamide (CAP) Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Most studied chemotherapy combination in ACC with ORR up to 33%; limited by retrospective data and small series; no phase III RCTs

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR ~25-33% but responses are typically not durable; no proven OS benefit; reserved for symptomatic metastatic disease

accessibility.rationale: All components widely available; standard chemotherapy agents at any oncology center

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity including nephrotoxicity, cardiotoxicity, myelosuppression, nausea/vomiting; cumulative dose limits

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirements; applicable regardless of molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combination cytotoxic chemotherapy: cisplatin causes DNA crosslinks, doxorubicin intercalates DNA and inhibits topoisomerase II, cyclophosphamide alkylates DNA. The combination provides synergistic cytotoxic activity against rapidly dividing tumor cells.

key_evidence.study_name: Efficacy of cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, and cisplatin for ACC

treatments.name: Vorinostat (HDAC Inhibitor)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial showed 2 partial responses with exceptional durability (53 months in one case); preclinical data shows synergy with cisplatin in depleting ACC cancer stem cells

survival_benefit.rationale: Limited ORR but exceptional durability in responders (53-month PR); preclinical evidence of cancer stem cell depletion may address long-term recurrence risk

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for cutaneous T-cell lymphoma; off-label use possible; oral medication

safety_profile.rationale: Known toxicity profile from CTCL indication; fatigue, diarrhea, thrombocytopenia; generally manageable

biomarker_match.rationale: MYB-NFIB fusion drives epigenetic dysregulation that may be targetable by HDAC inhibition; preclinical rationale for MYB-driven ACC

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Vorinostat inhibits histone deacetylases (HDACs), leading to accumulation of acetylated histones and altered gene expression. In ACC, HDAC inhibition unlocks chromatin to sensitize cancer stem cells to apoptosis and induces tumor senescence, potentially targeting the MYB-driven transcriptional program.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 study of vorinostat in locally advanced, recurrent, or metastatic ACC

treatments.name: All-Trans Retinoic Acid (ATRA)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial at Dana-Farber with 18 patients; 61% stable disease; no objective responses but excellent disease stabilization with minimal toxicity

survival_benefit.rationale: No objective responses achieved; 61% SD rate suggests disease stabilization rather than regression; may slow indolent progression

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for APL; inexpensive oral medication; off-label use straightforward

safety_profile.rationale: Excellent safety profile; no important adverse effects observed in ACC trial; well-established toxicity profile from decades of APL use

biomarker_match.rationale: Retinoic acid pathway interacts with MYB transcriptional programs; preclinical rationale for MYB-NFIB fusion-positive ACC

treatments.mechanism_of_action: ATRA binds retinoic acid receptors (RARs) to modulate gene transcription, inducing cellular differentiation and growth arrest. In ACC, ATRA may counteract MYB-driven dedifferentiation by restoring normal differentiation programs and altering myoepithelial cell phenotype.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II trial of ATRA in advanced ACC

treatments.name: Brontictuzumab (Anti-NOTCH1 Monoclonal Antibody)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase I data with 11 ACC patients; 18% ORR (2 unconfirmed responses); 45% SD; limited by small sample and early-phase design

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS only 2.7 months; median DOR 1.7 months; inferior to AL101 in head-to-head comparison of NOTCH-targeted approaches

accessibility.rationale: Investigational only; limited clinical trial availability; development status uncertain

safety_profile.rationale: Manageable safety profile; GI toxicity typical of NOTCH pathway inhibition; limited safety data in ACC population

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets NOTCH1 receptor which is activated in this patient; mechanism-specific for the molecular alteration present

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Brontictuzumab is a monoclonal antibody that binds to the NOTCH1 receptor, blocking ligand-induced activation and preventing downstream signaling. This directly targets the NOTCH1 activation driving tumor growth in this ACC subtype.

key_evidence.study_name: Clinical Outcomes With Notch Inhibitors in Notch-Activated R/M ACC

treatments.name: Lenvatinib plus Pembrolizumab Combination

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II trial specifically in ACC; failed to show superiority over lenvatinib alone in ORR; combination did not improve outcomes over monotherapy

survival_benefit.rationale: No improvement over lenvatinib monotherapy; ACC is immunologically cold tumor with sparse lymphocytic infiltration limiting checkpoint inhibitor benefit

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs FDA-approved individually; combination use for ACC requires clinical trial or off-label access

safety_profile.rationale: 71% grade 3+ AEs; 16 patients had treatment interruptions or dose reductions; higher toxicity than lenvatinib alone without added efficacy

biomarker_match.rationale: ACC is typically PD-L1 low and immunologically cold; limited rationale for checkpoint inhibition in this molecular context

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combines lenvatinib's multi-kinase inhibition (VEGFR, FGFR, PDGFR, RET, KIT) with pembrolizumab's PD-1 checkpoint blockade. The rationale was that VEGF inhibition could modulate the tumor microenvironment to enhance immunotherapy response.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II study of lenvatinib plus pembrolizumab in progressive R/M ACC

treatments.name: PRMT5 Inhibitor (Experimental)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase I data showing 21% PR rate in ACC; very early clinical evidence but promising signal in a difficult-to-treat cancer

survival_benefit.rationale: 21% PR rate exceeds many other agents tested in ACC; too early to assess survival impact

accessibility.rationale: Early phase I; extremely limited access; investigational compound with restricted trial sites

safety_profile.rationale: Limited safety data; phase I dose-finding; PRMT5 inhibitors can cause thrombocytopenia and hematologic toxicity

biomarker_match.rationale: PRMT5 is involved in epigenetic regulation downstream of MYB; rationale for MYB-NFIB fusion-positive ACC

treatments.mechanism_of_action: PRMT5 (protein arginine methyltransferase 5) inhibitors block arginine methylation of histones and other proteins, disrupting epigenetic regulation of gene expression. In ACC, PRMT5 inhibition may counteract MYB-driven transcriptional programs that sustain tumor growth.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase I trial of PRMT5 inhibitor in salivary gland cancers

treatments.name: Axitinib plus Avelumab Combination

evidence_level.rationale: Phase II clinical trial data published in JCO; studied specifically in R/M ACC population

survival_benefit.rationale: Modest activity; axitinib monotherapy showed 9.1% ORR and 4.8 month PFS; combination data incrementally better

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs FDA-approved for other indications; clinical trial for ACC combination; off-label use possible

safety_profile.rationale: Combined TKI and checkpoint inhibitor toxicity; hypertension, immune-mediated AEs, fatigue

biomarker_match.rationale: No specific biomarker requirement; ACC is immunologically cold limiting checkpoint inhibitor efficacy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Combines axitinib's selective VEGFR1-3 inhibition with avelumab's PD-L1 checkpoint blockade. Aims to improve anti-tumor immunity by normalizing tumor vasculature while simultaneously blocking immune checkpoint-mediated T-cell suppression.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase II Clinical Trial of Axitinib and Avelumab in R/M ACC

clinical_trials.title: ACCURACY: Phase 2 Trial of AL101 in R/M ACC with Notch Activating Mutations
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 12% (9/77 PR), DCR 69%; median PFS 4.7 months

clinical_trials.title: Phase 2 Trial of Rivoceranib for R/M ACC (RM-202)
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 15.3% by investigator, 9.7% by BIRC; median PFS 9.0 months

clinical_trials.title: Lenvatinib and Pembrolizumab in Advanced Salivary Gland Cancers
clinical_trials.early_results: Failed to show superiority of combination over lenvatinib alone in ACC cohort

clinical_trials.title: Phase 1 Dose Escalation of Emiltatug Ledadotin (XMT-1660) in Advanced Solid Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: 55.6% ORR in ACC-I patients; highest response rate ever seen in ACC

clinical_trials.title: Phase II Trial of ATRA in Advanced ACC (Dana-Farber)
clinical_trials.early_results: 61% SD rate; no objective responses; excellent tolerability

clinical_trials.title: Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma and Carbon Ion Only Irradiation (ACCO) Trial
clinical_trials.early_results: Prospective randomized trial evaluating carbon ion RT alone vs photon/carbon combination

combination_strategies.rationale: Surgery alone has high local recurrence in PNI-positive ACC; adjuvant proton RT provides 95% 3-year LC with superior normal tissue sparing compared to photon RT, critical for 20+ year surveillance period
Preclinical data suggest NOTCH inhibitor resistance is mediated by anti-apoptotic BCL2 pathway; combination addresses intrinsic resistance mechanisms in NOTCH-activated ACC

Vorinostat unlocks chromatin to sensitize ACC cancer stem cells to cisplatin; combination was effective in depleting cancer stem cells and reducing tumor viability through cellular senescence induction
Targeting both VEGFR-driven angiogenesis and NOTCH1 oncogenic signaling simultaneously in NOTCH1-activated ACC; addresses two key molecular drivers with non-overlapping mechanisms
Concurrent cisplatin enhances radiosensitivity; series of 9 patients with unresectable ACC showed feasibility; may improve locoregional control in high-risk PNI-positive tumors

supportive_care.approach: Lingual Nerve Rehabilitation and Sensory Retraining
supportive_care.evidence: Clinical practice guidelines for post-glossectomy rehabilitation; occupational therapy protocols for sensory re-education
supportive_care.benefit: Improved adaptation to altered tongue sensation and taste; enhanced swallowing function and quality of life after lingual nerve sacrifice

supportive_care.evidence: Standard of care for floor of mouth surgery; speech-language pathology intervention guidelines
supportive_care.benefit: Recovery of speech intelligibility and safe swallowing function; prevention of aspiration; improved nutritional status

supportive_care.evidence: ACC literature demonstrating late recurrences and distant metastases occurring 10-20+ years post-treatment; 20-year OS drops to 15-28%
supportive_care.benefit: Early detection of late recurrences and lung metastases; serial imaging with chest CT and MRI; enables timely intervention for recurrence

supportive_care.approach: Dental Rehabilitation and Oral Care Post-Radiation
supportive_care.evidence: NCCN supportive care guidelines for head and neck cancer patients receiving radiation therapy
supportive_care.benefit: Prevention of osteoradionecrosis; management of xerostomia; fluoride therapy to prevent radiation caries; regular dental monitoring

supportive_care.approach: Psychosocial Support and Quality of Life Management
supportive_care.evidence: Head and neck cancer quality of life studies; EORTC QLQ-H&N35 validated outcomes
supportive_care.benefit: Addressing altered appearance, speech, swallowing, and taste changes; coping with long surveillance period; anxiety management for disease monitoring

supportive_care.evidence: Evidence-based nutrition guidelines for head and neck cancer patients post-surgery and radiation
supportive_care.benefit: Maintaining adequate nutrition during and after treatment; adapting diet to swallowing limitations; preventing weight loss and malnutrition
